content.text:
Question:
                                How many days ago did I attend a baking class at a local culinary school when I made my friend's birthday cake?

                                Question date:
                                2022/04/15 (Fri) 14:21

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                21 days. 22 days (including the last day) is also acceptable.

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:9a707b81
            Context mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-54be40fbad58-claim-4 [ACTIVE] attended_event family=attended_event: The user attended a baking class at a local culinary school on 2022-03-20.; scope=User's attended events; context="The user described the baking class as inspiration for wanting to make strawberry shortcake."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:1689b2cc05a2b917f6172b33c1fbf4a7 quote="baking class I took at a local culinary school yesterday"
- claim hot-path-54be40fbad58-claim-3 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user took a baking class at a local culinary school on 2022-03-20.; scope=User's baking experience; context="The user said this while discussing inspiration for a strawberry shortcake baking project."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:1689b2cc05a2b917f6172b33c1fbf4a7 quote="that amazing baking class I took at a local culinary school yesterday"
- claim hot-path-01a15d5f5207-claim-16 [ACTIVE] attended_event family=attended_event: The user baked a chocolate cake for a friend's birthday party on 2022-04-10.; scope=User's baking activity and birthday-party context on 2022-04-10; context="The chocolate cake used espresso powder to intensify the chocolate flavor and turned out amazing."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:2d2385052cc88974c2d903c5b80aaf2b quote="I just baked a chocolate cake for my friend's birthday party today that turned out amazing."
- claim hot-path-01a15d5f5207-claim-17 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user used espresso powder in a chocolate cake recipe to intensify the chocolate flavor.; scope=User's ingredient experience in chocolate cake on 2022-04-10; context="The user found the new recipe online, and the cake turned out amazing for a friend's birthday party on 2022-04-10."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:2d2385052cc88974c2d903c5b80aaf2b quote="used espresso powder to intensify the chocolate flavor"
- claim hot-path-54be40fbad58-claim-11 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has used oat flour to make pancakes and waffles and found that it gives them a nice nutty flavor.; scope=User's baking experience with oat flour; context="The user mentioned this while considering oat flour or bread flour for sourdough shortcake."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:1689b2cc05a2b917f6172b33c1fbf4a7 quote="I've also been using oat flour to make pancakes and waffles, and it gives them a nice nutty flavor"
- claim hot-path-01a15d5f5207-claim-13 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user's last attempt at making croissants did not turn out well before 2022-04-10.; scope=User's croissant baking experience before the 2022-04-10 imported session; context="The user still planned to try making croissants from scratch for the dinner party."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:2d2385052cc88974c2d903c5b80aaf2b quote="my last attempt didn't turn out so well"
- claim hot-path-01a15d5f5207-claim-8 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user made a strawberry rhubarb crisp a few weeks before 2022-04-10 and thought it was incredible.; scope=User's baking experience before the 2022-04-10 imported session; context="The user described the strawberry rhubarb crisp while discussing recent flavor experiments and dinner-party desserts."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:2d2385052cc88974c2d903c5b80aaf2b quote="a strawberry rhubarb crisp I made a few weeks ago was incredible"
- claim hot-path-01a15d5f5207-claim-15 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user recently made banana bread with walnuts, and it turned out amazing.; scope=User's banana-bread baking experience before the 2022-04-10 imported session; context="The user mentioned this while asking for tips for making moist and flavorful banana bread."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:2d2385052cc88974c2d903c5b80aaf2b quote="I recently made a batch with walnuts, and it turned out amazing."

            Retrieved notes:
            - note hot-path-01a15d5f5207-note-3 [CONTEXT/ACTIVE]: User's practical baking lessons from recent successes and attempts; By 2022-04-10, the user had recent practical baking experience: a strawberry rhubarb crisp was considered incredible, lemon bars with shortbread crust and fresh-lemon egg curd turned out tangy and sweet, walnut banana bread turned out amazing, and a chocolate cake with espresso powder for a friend's birthday turned out amazing. The user also wanted to retry croissants from scratch after a previous attempt did not go well.; scope=User's recent baking experiences and lessons discussed on 2022-04-10; evidence=SUMMARIZED source=external:provided-text:2d2385052cc88974c2d903c5b80aaf2b quote="I recently made a batch with walnuts, and it turned out amazing. By the way, I just baked a chocolate cake for my friend's birthday party today that turned out amazing."

LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2022/03/21 (Mon) 01:22
Transcript:
user: I'm looking for some inspiration for my next baking project. I've been really into baking lately.
assistant: What type of baking projects are you interested in tackling next? Are you looking for something sweet, savory, or perhaps something specific like bread, pastries, or cakes?

If you're open to suggestions, I can offer a few ideas to get you started:

1. **Flavored Cakes**: Consider experimenting with unique flavor combinations like lemon-rosemary, orange-almond, or chocolate-chipotle....
...3. **French Pastries**: Delve into the world of croissants, pain au chocolat, or eclairs. These classic treats require some skill, but the end result is well worth the effort.
4. **Seasonal Treats**: Create something inspired by the current season. For example, make a pumpkin spice cake in the fall, a citrusy tart in the winter, or a fresh berry pie in the summer....
...user: I've been obsessed with strawberries lately, especially after that amazing baking class I took at a local culinary school yesterday. I was thinking of making a strawberry shortcake. Do you have any tips on how to make the perfect shortcake?
assistant: That sounds like a wonderful experience! It's great that you're feeling inspired to keep baking and exploring new recipes. Strawberry shortcake is a classic! It's a perfect way to showcase fresh strawberries, and with a few tips, you can create a truly unforgettable dessert. Here are some secrets to making the perfect shortcake:...
...3. **Use cold ingredients**: Cold butter, cold cream, and cold eggs will help create a flaky, tender shortcake.
4. **Don't overbake**: Shortcake should be lightly golden and still tender in the center. Overbaking will make it dry and crumbly....
...5. Roll the dough out to about 1 inch (2.5 cm) thickness. Use a biscuit cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out shortcakes. Gather the scraps, re-roll, and cut out more shortcakes.
6. Place the shortcakes on the prepared baking sheet, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of space between each....
...10. Spoon sliced strawberries over the bottom half of each shortcake, followed by a dollop of whipped cream. Top with the top half of the shortcake and dust with confectioner's sugar....
...user: That's great advice on whipping cream! I've been experimenting with sourdough bread lately, and I was thinking of using some of my sourdough starter to make a sourdough shortcake instead of regular shortcake. Do you think that would work?...
...6. **Monitor the texture**: Keep an eye on the shortcake's texture during the proofing and baking process. Sourdough can be more delicate, so you may need to adjust the baking time to prevent overcooking....
...7. Place the shortcakes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of space between each....
...user: I'm thinking of trying a different type of flour for my sourdough shortcake.  I've been experimenting with different types of flour lately, and I've been loving the results. I made a batch of cookies with bread flour, and they turned out chewier than usual. I've also been using oat flour to make pancakes and waffles, and it gives them a nice nutty flavor. Do you think I could use oat flour or bread flour for my sourdough shortcake?...
...Oat flour can add a delightful nutty flavor and a slightly denser texture to your shortcake. However, keep in mind that oat flour is a coarser, whole-grain flour that can affect the overall structure and rise of your shortcake. Oat flour also has a lower gluten content than all-purpose flour, which might impact the shortcake's texture and crumb....
...Bread flour, on the other hand, is a high-protein flour that can add strength and structure to your shortcake. It's a great choice if you want a more tender, chewy crumb. However, bread flour can also make the shortcake more dense and heavier than all-purpose flour....
- source external:provided-text:2d2385052cc88974c2d903c5b80aaf2b [external_record ref=longmemeval:9a707b81:session:2 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2022/04/10 (Sun) 13:30
Transcript:
user: I'm looking for some new recipe ideas for a dinner party I'm hosting next weekend. I've been really into baking lately. Do you have any suggestions for a unique dessert I could make for my dinner party?...
...10. **Espresso Granita**: End the meal with a refreshing, icy treat. This Italian-style granita is made with espresso, sugar, and water, then scraped with a fork to create a slushy, crystalline texture....
...user: I love these ideas, especially the matcha tiramisu and salted caramel chocolate tart. I've been experimenting with different flavors lately, like strawberries, and I think a strawberry rhubarb crisp I made a few weeks ago was incredible. Do you have any ideas for a savory dish that would complement these sweet treats?...
...4. **Grilled Asparagus with Lemon-Parmesan Vinaigrette**: Asparagus is a natural pairing with rich desserts. Grill it to perfection and serve with a light, zesty vinaigrette made with lemon juice, olive oil, and Parmesan cheese....
...These ideas should provide a nice balance to your sweet treats. You can't go wrong with a classic grilled chicken or scallops, but if you want to offer something a bit more adventurous, the roasted vegetable tart or herbed quinoa salad could be great options....
...6. **Egg Salad with Pickled Carrots and Chives**: A classic egg salad with a twist! The pickled carrots will add a nice acidity, while the chives will provide a fresh, oniony flavor....
...These fillings offer a mix of sweet, savory, tangy, and spicy flavors that will complement the matcha tiramisu and salted caramel chocolate tart nicely. You can't go wrong with a classic combination like smoked salmon or caramelized onion and brie, but feel free to experiment with the other options to find the perfect fit for your dinner party!
user: I'm also thinking of making some lemon bars for the dinner party. Do you have any tips for making a shortbread crust? I recently made a batch with a shortbread crust and a lemon curd made with fresh lemons and eggs, and they turned out tangy and sweet.
assistant: Lemon bars are a great addition to your dinner party! A shortbread crust is a classic base for lemon bars, and with a few tips, you can achieve a buttery, crumbly, and tender crust. Here are some tips to help you make a perfect shortbread crust:...
...5. **Don't overbake**: Shortbread crusts can go from perfect to burnt quickly. Keep an eye on the crust while it's baking, and remove it from the oven when it's lightly golden brown....
...7. **Chill the crust**: Chill the crust in the pan for at least 30 minutes before baking to allow the butter to firm up and the gluten to relax....
...user: I'm excited to try making croissants again, and I think I'll also make some banana bread for the dinner party. I recently made a batch with walnuts, and it turned out amazing. By the way, I just baked a chocolate cake for my friend's birthday party today that turned out amazing. It was a new recipe I found online that used espresso powder to intensify the chocolate flavor. Anyways, do you have any tips for making a moist and flavorful banana bread?
assistant: Congratulations on the success of your chocolate cake! Using espresso powder is a great way to enhance the flavor of chocolate. Banana bread is a great addition to your dinner party! With a few tips, you can ensure your banana bread turns out moist and flavorful. Here are some tips to help you achieve banana bread perfection:...
...4. **Don't overbake**: Banana bread is best when it's baked until it's just golden brown and still slightly tender in the center. Overbaking can cause the bread to dry out....

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [CONCEPT]: Baking class; Concept named Baking class.
- entity entity:<1> [CONCEPT]: Baking; Concept named Baking.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

hypothesis.description: Short answer that can be produced from the retrieved memory context.

reason.description: One short sentence explaining the judgement.

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:

Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.

Transcript:
user: I'm looking for some inspiration for my next baking project. I've been really into baking lately.
assistant: What type of baking projects are you interested in tackling next? Are you looking for something sweet, savory, or perhaps something specific like bread, pastries, or cakes?

If you're open to suggestions, I can offer a few ideas to get you started:

1. **Flavored Cakes**: Consider experimenting with unique flavor combinations like lemon-rosemary, orange-almond, or chocolate-chipotle....
...3. **French Pastries**: Delve into the world of croissants, pain au chocolat, or eclairs. These classic treats require some skill, but the end result is well worth the effort.
4. **Seasonal Treats**: Create something inspired by the current season. For example, make a pumpkin spice cake in the fall, a citrusy tart in the winter, or a fresh berry pie in the summer....
...user: I've been obsessed with strawberries lately, especially after that amazing baking class I took at a local culinary school yesterday. I was thinking of making a strawberry shortcake. Do you have any tips on how to make the perfect shortcake?
assistant: That sounds like a wonderful experience! It's great that you're feeling inspired to keep baking and exploring new recipes. Strawberry shortcake is a classic! It's a perfect way to showcase fresh strawberries, and with a few tips, you can create a truly unforgettable dessert. Here are some secrets to making the perfect shortcake:...
...3. **Use cold ingredients**: Cold butter, cold cream, and cold eggs will help create a flaky, tender shortcake.
4. **Don't overbake**: Shortcake should be lightly golden and still tender in the center. Overbaking will make it dry and crumbly....
...5. Roll the dough out to about 1 inch (2.5 cm) thickness. Use a biscuit cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out shortcakes. Gather the scraps, re-roll, and cut out more shortcakes.
6. Place the shortcakes on the prepared baking sheet, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of space between each....
...10. Spoon sliced strawberries over the bottom half of each shortcake, followed by a dollop of whipped cream. Top with the top half of the shortcake and dust with confectioner's sugar....
...user: That's great advice on whipping cream! I've been experimenting with sourdough bread lately, and I was thinking of using some of my sourdough starter to make a sourdough shortcake instead of regular shortcake. Do you think that would work?...
...6. **Monitor the texture**: Keep an eye on the shortcake's texture during the proofing and baking process. Sourdough can be more delicate, so you may need to adjust the baking time to prevent overcooking....
...7. Place the shortcakes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of space between each....
...user: I'm thinking of trying a different type of flour for my sourdough shortcake.  I've been experimenting with different types of flour lately, and I've been loving the results. I made a batch of cookies with bread flour, and they turned out chewier than usual. I've also been using oat flour to make pancakes and waffles, and it gives them a nice nutty flavor. Do you think I could use oat flour or bread flour for my sourdough shortcake?...
...Oat flour can add a delightful nutty flavor and a slightly denser texture to your shortcake. However, keep in mind that oat flour is a coarser, whole-grain flour that can affect the overall structure and rise of your shortcake. Oat flour also has a lower gluten content than all-purpose flour, which might impact the shortcake's texture and crumb....
...Bread flour, on the other hand, is a high-protein flour that can add strength and structure to your shortcake. It's a great choice if you want a more tender, chewy crumb. However, bread flour can also make the shortcake more dense and heavier than all-purpose flour....
